summary: A man with the cognomen Rex, recipient of Fam. 13.52 (opener CICERO REGI S.), recommended A. Licinius Aristoteles of Malta. Identification uncertain; likely Q. Marcius Rex, but the cognomen alone is the only firm datum.

A Maltese (Melitensis) who was Cicero's oldest guest-friend (hospes antiquissimus) and a close intimate; he had taken Cicero's side in the civil war even longer than Cicero himself, and obtained pardon from Caesar through Cicero's intercession. Subject of the recommendation Fam. 13.52 (46 BC).

A long-standing intimate of Cicero, described as an excellent and most grateful man, with business interests and landholdings in the Hellespont (around Parium). Subject of the recommendation Fam. 13.53 to Q. Minucius Thermus, propraetor of Asia (51-50 BC).

A Greek city on the Hellespont (the Propontis coast of the Troad), in the Roman province of Asia. Its civic community (Pariana civitas) had decreed and granted land rights to L. Genucilius Curvus (Fam. 13.53).

Cicero's friend and interpreter (interpres) during his Cilician proconsulship (51-50 BC), of singular and almost incredible loyalty, restraint and modesty, noted through his long term of attendance (apparitio). Father of M. Marcilius, on whose behalf Cicero wrote Fam. 13.54 to Q. Minucius Thermus.

Son of Cicero's friend and interpreter Marcilius; treated generously by Q. Minucius Thermus on Cicero's recommendation and came to thank him at Laodicea. Subject of Fam. 13.54, in which Cicero further asks that the young man's mother-in-law not be made a defendant.

aliases: Gaius Titius Rufus, son of Lucius
summary: Gaius Titius Rufus, son of Lucius, praetor urbanus (city praetor) at Rome ca. 50 BC; recipient of Fam. 13.58, in which Cicero commends his fellow-tribesman L. Custidius for a case to be heard at the praetor's tribunal. Possibly identical with person:c-titius, flagged for a later merge decision.

Cicero's fellow tribesman (tribulis), fellow townsman (municeps) and close friend, with a case to bring before the city praetor C. Titius Rufus. Subject of the recommendation Fam. 13.58 (ca. 50 BC); otherwise unknown.
